texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 6
texts.text: Article 6

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Lawfulness of processing
texts.text: Lawfulness of processing

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies:
texts.text: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   the  data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her  personal data for one or  more specific purposes;
texts.text: (a)   the  data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her  personal data for one or  more specific purposes;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  performance of a contract  to which the data subject  is party or  in  order  to take steps at  the request of  the data subject prior  to entering into a contract;
texts.text: (b)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  performance of a contract  to which the data subject  is party or  in  order  to take steps at  the request of  the data subject prior  to entering into a contract;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which the controller is subject;
texts.text: (c)   processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation to which the controller is subject;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d)   processing is necessary in order  to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another  natural person;
texts.text: (d)   processing is necessary in order  to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another  natural person;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  or  in  the  exercise  of  official authority vested in the controller;
texts.text: (e)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  or  in  the  exercise  of  official authority vested in the controller;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  the  legitimate  interests  pursued  by  the  controller  or  by  a  third  party, except  where  such  interests  are  overridden  by  the  interests  or  fundamental  rights  and  freedoms  of  the  data  subject which require protection of personal data, in particular  where the data subject is a child.
texts.text: (f)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  the  legitimate  interests  pursued  by  the  controller  or  by  a  third  party, except  where  such  interests  are  overridden  by  the  interests  or  fundamental  rights  and  freedoms  of  the  data  subject which require protection of personal data, in particular  where the data subject is a child.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Point  (f)  of  the  first  subparagraph  shall  not  apply  to  processing  carried  out  by  public  authorities  in  the  performance  of their  tasks.
texts.text: Point  (f)  of  the  first  subparagraph  shall  not  apply  to  processing  carried  out  by  public  authorities  in  the  performance  of their  tasks.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member  States  may  maintain  or  introduce  more  specific  provisions  to  adapt  the  application  of  the  rules  of  this Regulation  with  regard  to  processing  for  compliance  with  points  (c)  and  (e)  of  paragraph  1  by  determining  more precisely  specific  requirements  for  the  processing  and  other  measures  to  ensure  lawful  and  fair  processing  including  for other specific processing situations as provided for in Chapter IX.
texts.text: Member  States  may  maintain  or  introduce  more  specific  provisions  to  adapt  the  application  of  the  rules  of  this Regulation  with  regard  to  processing  for  compliance  with  points  (c)  and  (e)  of  paragraph  1  by  determining  more precisely  specific  requirements  for  the  processing  and  other  measures  to  ensure  lawful  and  fair  processing  including  for other specific processing situations as provided for in Chapter IX.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The basis for  the processing referred to in point (c) and (e) of paragraph 1 shall be laid down by:
texts.text: The basis for  the processing referred to in point (c) and (e) of paragraph 1 shall be laid down by:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   Union law; or
texts.text: (a)   Union law; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   Member State law to which the controller is subject.
texts.text: (b)   Member State law to which the controller is subject.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of the processing shall be determined in that legal basis or, as regards the processing referred to in point (e) of  paragraph  1,  shall  be  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  or  in  the  exercise  of official  authority  vested  in  the  controller.  That  legal  basis  may  contain  specific  provisions  to  adapt  the  application  of rules  of  this  Regulation,  inter  alia:  the  general  conditions  governing  the  lawfulness  of  processing  by  the  controller;  the types  of  data  which  are  subject  to  the  processing;  the  data  subjects  concerned;  the  entities  to,  and  the  purposes  for which,  the  personal  data  may  be  disclosed;  the  purpose  limitation;  storage  periods;  and  processing  operations  and processing  procedures,  including  measures  to  ensure  lawful  and  fair processing  such  as  those  for  other  specific processing  situations  as  provided  for  in  Chapter  IX.  The  Union  or  the  Member  State  law  shall  meet  an  objective  of public interest and be proportionate to the legitimate aim pursued.
texts.text: The purpose of the processing shall be determined in that legal basis or, as regards the processing referred to in point (e) of  paragraph  1,  shall  be  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  or  in  the  exercise  of official  authority  vested  in  the  controller.  That  legal  basis  may  contain  specific  provisions  to  adapt  the  application  of rules  of  this  Regulation,  inter  alia:  the  general  conditions  governing  the  lawfulness  of  processing  by  the  controller;  the types  of  data  which  are  subject  to  the  processing;  the  data  subjects  concerned;  the  entities  to,  and  the  purposes  for which,  the  personal  data  may  be  disclosed;  the  purpose  limitation;  storage  periods;  and  processing  operations  and processing  procedures,  including  measures  to  ensure  lawful  and  fair processing  such  as  those  for  other  specific processing  situations  as  provided  for  in  Chapter  IX.  The  Union  or  the  Member  State  law  shall  meet  an  objective  of public interest and be proportionate to the legitimate aim pursued.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the  processing  for  a  purpose  other  than  that  for  which  the  personal  data  have  been  collected  is  not  based on  the  data  subject's  consent  or  on  a  Union  or  Member  State  law  which  constitutes  a  necessary  and  proportionate measure  in  a  democratic  society  to  safeguard  the  objectives  referred  to  in  Article  23(1),  the  controller  shall,  in  order  to ascertain  whether  processing  for  another  purpose  is  compatible  with  the  purpose  for  which  the  personal  data  are initially collected, take into account, inter alia:
texts.text: Where the  processing  for  a  purpose  other  than  that  for  which  the  personal  data  have  been  collected  is  not  based on  the  data  subject's  consent  or  on  a  Union  or  Member  State  law  which  constitutes  a  necessary  and  proportionate measure  in  a  democratic  society  to  safeguard  the  objectives  referred  to  in  Article  23(1),  the  controller  shall,  in  order  to ascertain  whether  processing  for  another  purpose  is  compatible  with  the  purpose  for  which  the  personal  data  are initially collected, take into account, inter alia:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   any  link  between  the  purposes  for  which  the  personal  data  have  been  collected  and  the  purposes  of  the  intended further processing;
texts.text: (a)   any  link  between  the  purposes  for  which  the  personal  data  have  been  collected  and  the  purposes  of  the  intended further processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   the  context  in  which  the  personal  data  have  been  collected,  in  particular  regarding  the  relationship  between  data subjects and the controller;
texts.text: (b)   the  context  in  which  the  personal  data  have  been  collected,  in  particular  regarding  the  relationship  between  data subjects and the controller;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   the  nature  of  the  personal  data,  in  particular  whether  special  categories  of  personal  data  are  processed,  pursuant  to Article  9,  or  whether  personal  data  related  to  criminal  convictions  and  offences  are  processed,  pursuant  to  Article 10;
texts.text: (c)   the  nature  of  the  personal  data,  in  particular  whether  special  categories  of  personal  data  are  processed,  pursuant  to Article  9,  or  whether  personal  data  related  to  criminal  convictions  and  offences  are  processed,  pursuant  to  Article 10;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d)   the  possible consequences of  the intended further processing for data subjects;
texts.text: (d)   the  possible consequences of  the intended further processing for data subjects;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e)   the  existence of appropriate safeguards, which may include encryption or pseudonymisation.
texts.text: (e)   the  existence of appropriate safeguards, which may include encryption or pseudonymisation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 8
texts.text: Article 8

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Conditions applicable to child's consent in relation to information society services
texts.text: Conditions applicable to child's consent in relation to information society services

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where  point  (a)  of  Article  6(1)  applies,  in  relation  to  the  offer  of  information  society  services  directly  to  a  child, the  processing  of  the  personal  data  of  a  child  shall  be  lawful  where  the  child  is  at  least  16  years  old.  Where  the  child  is below  the  age  of  16  years,  such  processing  shall  be  lawful  only  if  and  to  the  extent  that  consent  is  given  or  authorised by the holder of parental responsibility over  the child.
texts.text: Where  point  (a)  of  Article  6(1)  applies,  in  relation  to  the  offer  of  information  society  services  directly  to  a  child, the  processing  of  the  personal  data  of  a  child  shall  be  lawful  where  the  child  is  at  least  16  years  old.  Where  the  child  is below  the  age  of  16  years,  such  processing  shall  be  lawful  only  if  and  to  the  extent  that  consent  is  given  or  authorised by the holder of parental responsibility over  the child.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Member  States  may  provide  by  law  for  a  lower  age  for  those  purposes  provided  that  such  lower  age  is  not  below  13 years.
texts.text: Member  States  may  provide  by  law  for  a  lower  age  for  those  purposes  provided  that  such  lower  age  is  not  below  13 years.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  controller  shall  make  reasonable  efforts  to  verify  in  such  cases  that  consent  is  given  or  authorised  by  the holder of parental responsibility over  the child, taking into consideration available technology.
texts.text: The  controller  shall  make  reasonable  efforts  to  verify  in  such  cases  that  consent  is  given  or  authorised  by  the holder of parental responsibility over  the child, taking into consideration available technology.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraph 1 shall  not  affect  the  general  contract  law of  Member  States  such  as  the  rules  on  the  validity,  formation or effect of a contract in relation to a child.
texts.text: Paragraph 1 shall  not  affect  the  general  contract  law of  Member  States  such  as  the  rules  on  the  validity,  formation or effect of a contract in relation to a child.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 9
texts.text: Article 9

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processing of special categories of personal data
texts.text: Processing of special categories of personal data

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or  philosophical beliefs, or trade  union  membership,  and  the  processing  of  genetic  data,  biometric  data  for  the  purpose  of  uniquely  identifying  a natural  person,  data  concerning  health  or  data  concerning  a  natural  person's  sex  life  or  sexual  orientation  shall  be prohibited.
texts.text: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or  philosophical beliefs, or trade  union  membership,  and  the  processing  of  genetic  data,  biometric  data  for  the  purpose  of  uniquely  identifying  a natural  person,  data  concerning  health  or  data  concerning  a  natural  person's  sex  life  or  sexual  orientation  shall  be prohibited.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies:
texts.text: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   the  data  subject  has  given  explicit  consent  to  the  processing  of  those  personal  data  for  one  or  more  specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject;
texts.text: (a)   the  data  subject  has  given  explicit  consent  to  the  processing  of  those  personal  data  for  one  or  more  specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  carrying  out  the  obligations  and  exercising  specific  rights  of  the controller  or  of  the  data  subject  in  the  field  of  employment  and  social  security  and  social  protection  law  in  so  far as it  is  authorised by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement pursuant to Member State law providing for appropriate safeguards for  the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;
texts.text: (b)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  carrying  out  the  obligations  and  exercising  specific  rights  of  the controller  or  of  the  data  subject  in  the  field  of  employment  and  social  security  and  social  protection  law  in  so  far as it  is  authorised by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement pursuant to Member State law providing for appropriate safeguards for  the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   processing  is  necessary  to  protect  the  vital  interests  of  the  data  subject  or  of  another  natural  person  where  the  data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent;
texts.text: (c)   processing  is  necessary  to  protect  the  vital  interests  of  the  data  subject  or  of  another  natural  person  where  the  data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d)   processing  is  carried  out  in  the  course  of  its  legitimate  activities  with  appropriate  safeguards  by  a  foundation, association  or  any  other  not-for-profit  body  with  a  political,  philosophical,  religious  or  trade  union  aim  and  on condition  that  the  processing  relates  solely  to  the  members  or  to  former  members  of  the  body  or  to  persons  who have regular contact with it in connection with its purposes and that the personal data are not disclosed outside that body without the consent of the data subjects;
texts.text: (d)   processing  is  carried  out  in  the  course  of  its  legitimate  activities  with  appropriate  safeguards  by  a  foundation, association  or  any  other  not-for-profit  body  with  a  political,  philosophical,  religious  or  trade  union  aim  and  on condition  that  the  processing  relates  solely  to  the  members  or  to  former  members  of  the  body  or  to  persons  who have regular contact with it in connection with its purposes and that the personal data are not disclosed outside that body without the consent of the data subjects;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e)   processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;
texts.text: (e)   processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  establishment,  exercise  or  defence  of  legal  claims  or  whenever  courts  are  acting  in their  judicial capacity;
texts.text: (f)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  establishment,  exercise  or  defence  of  legal  claims  or  whenever  courts  are  acting  in their  judicial capacity;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g)   processing  is  necessary  for  reasons  of  substantial  public  interest,  on  the  basis  of  Union  or  Member  State  law  which shall  be  proportionate  to  the  aim  pursued,  respect  the  essence  of  the  right  to  data  protection  and  provide  for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;
texts.text: (g)   processing  is  necessary  for  reasons  of  substantial  public  interest,  on  the  basis  of  Union  or  Member  State  law  which shall  be  proportionate  to  the  aim  pursued,  respect  the  essence  of  the  right  to  data  protection  and  provide  for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  preventive  or  occupational  medicine,  for  the  assessment  of  the  working capacity of  the  employee,  medical  diagnosis,  the  provision  of  health  or  social  care  or  treatment  or  the  management of  health  or  social  care  systems  and  services  on  the  basis  of  Union  or  Member  State  law  or  pursuant  to  contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3;
texts.text: (h)   processing  is  necessary  for  the  purposes  of  preventive  or  occupational  medicine,  for  the  assessment  of  the  working capacity of  the  employee,  medical  diagnosis,  the  provision  of  health  or  social  care  or  treatment  or  the  management of  health  or  social  care  systems  and  services  on  the  basis  of  Union  or  Member  State  law  or  pursuant  to  contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) processing  is  necessary  for  reasons  of  public  interest  in  the  area  of  public  health,  such  as  protecting  against  serious cross-border  threats  to  health  or  ensuring  high  standards  of  quality  and  safety  of  health  care  and  of  medicinal products  or  medical  devices,  on  the  basis  of  Union  or  Member  State  law  which  provides  for  suitable  and  specific measures to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject, in particular professional secrecy;
texts.text: (i) processing  is  necessary  for  reasons  of  public  interest  in  the  area  of  public  health,  such  as  protecting  against  serious cross-border  threats  to  health  or  ensuring  high  standards  of  quality  and  safety  of  health  care  and  of  medicinal products  or  medical  devices,  on  the  basis  of  Union  or  Member  State  law  which  provides  for  suitable  and  specific measures to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject, in particular professional secrecy;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) processing  is  necessary  for  archiving  purposes  in  the  public  interest,  scientific  or  historical  research  purposes  or statistical  purposes  in  accordance  with  Article  89(1)  based  on  Union  or  Member  State  law  which  shall  be  propor­ tionate  to  the  aim  pursued,  respect  the  essence  of  the  right  to  data  protection  and  provide  for  suitable  and  specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject.
texts.text: (j) processing  is  necessary  for  archiving  purposes  in  the  public  interest,  scientific  or  historical  research  purposes  or statistical  purposes  in  accordance  with  Article  89(1)  based  on  Union  or  Member  State  law  which  shall  be  propor­ tionate  to  the  aim  pursued,  respect  the  essence  of  the  right  to  data  protection  and  provide  for  suitable  and  specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for  the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those  data  are  processed  by or  under  the  responsibility  of  a  professional  subject  to  the  obligation  of  professional secrecy  under  Union  or  Member  State  law  or  rules  established  by  national  competent  bodies  or  by  another  person  also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies.
texts.text: Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for  the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those  data  are  processed  by or  under  the  responsibility  of  a  professional  subject  to  the  obligation  of  professional secrecy  under  Union  or  Member  State  law  or  rules  established  by  national  competent  bodies  or  by  another  person  also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States  may  maintain  or  introduce  further  conditions,  including  limitations,  with  regard  to  the  processing of genetic data, biometric data or data concerning health.
texts.text: Member States  may  maintain  or  introduce  further  conditions,  including  limitations,  with  regard  to  the  processing of genetic data, biometric data or data concerning health.

texts.orig: Article 10
texts.text: Article 10

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences
texts.text: Processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Processing  of  personal  data  relating  to  criminal  convictions  and  offences  or related  security  measures  based  on Article  6(1)  shall  be  carried  out  only  under  the  control  of  official  authority  or  when  the  processing  is  authorised  by Union  or  Member  State  law  providing  for  appropriate  safeguards  for  the  rights  and  freedoms  of  data  subjects.  Any comprehensive register of criminal convictions shall be kept only under  the control of official authority.
texts.text: Processing  of  personal  data  relating  to  criminal  convictions  and  offences  or related  security  measures  based  on Article  6(1)  shall  be  carried  out  only  under  the  control  of  official  authority  or  when  the  processing  is  authorised  by Union  or  Member  State  law  providing  for  appropriate  safeguards  for  the  rights  and  freedoms  of  data  subjects.  Any comprehensive register of criminal convictions shall be kept only under  the control of official authority.

texts.orig: Article 13
texts.text: Article 13

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Information to be provided where personal data are collected from the data subject
texts.text: Information to be provided where personal data are collected from the data subject

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal  data  relating  to  a  data  subject  are  collected  from  the  data  subject,  the  controller  shall,  at  the  time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with all of the following information:
texts.text: Where personal  data  relating  to  a  data  subject  are  collected  from  the  data  subject,  the  controller  shall,  at  the  time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with all of the following information:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   the  identity  and the contact details of  the controller and, where applicable, of  the controller's representative;
texts.text: (a)   the  identity  and the contact details of  the controller and, where applicable, of  the controller's representative;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   the  contact details of  the data protection officer, where applicable;
texts.text: (b)   the  contact details of  the data protection officer, where applicable;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   the  purposes of  the processing for  which the personal data are intended as well as the legal basis for  the processing;
texts.text: (c)   the  purposes of  the processing for  which the personal data are intended as well as the legal basis for  the processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d)   where  the  processing  is  based  on  point  (f)  of  Article  6(1),  the  legitimate  interests  pursued  by  the  controller  or  by  a third party;
texts.text: (d)   where  the  processing  is  based  on  point  (f)  of  Article  6(1),  the  legitimate  interests  pursued  by  the  controller  or  by  a third party;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e)   the  recipients or categories of recipients of  the personal data, if any;
texts.text: (e)   the  recipients or categories of recipients of  the personal data, if any;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f)   where  applicable,  the  fact  that  the  controller  intends  to  transfer  personal  data  to  a  third  country  or  international organisation  and  the  existence  or  absence  of  an  adequacy  decision  by  the  Commission,  or  in  the  case  of  transfers referred  to in  Article  46  or  47,  or  the  second  subparagraph  of  Article  49(1),  reference  to the  appropriate  or  suitable safeguards and the means by which to obtain a copy of them or where they have been made available.

texts.text: (b)   for  compliance  with  a  legal  obligation  which  requires  processing  by  Union  or  Member  State  law  to  which  the controller  is  subject  or  for  the  performance  of  a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  or  in  the  exercise  of  official authority vested in the controller;

texts.text: (c)   for  reasons  of  public  interest  in  the  area  of  public  health  in  accordance  with  points  (h)  and  (i)  of  Article  9(2)  as  well as Article 9(3);

texts.text: (d)   for  archiving  purposes  in  the  public  interest,  scientific  or  historical  research  purposes  or  statistical  purposes  in accordance  with  Article  89(1)  in  so  far  as  the  right  referred  to  in  paragraph  1  is  likely  to  render  impossible  or seriously impair the achievement of the objectives of that processing; or

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to restriction of processing
texts.text: Right to restriction of processing

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to  obtain  from  the  controller  restriction  of  processing  where  one  of  the following applies:
texts.text: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to  obtain  from  the  controller  restriction  of  processing  where  one  of  the following applies:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   the  accuracy  of  the  personal  data  is  contested  by  the  data  subject,  for  a  period  enabling  the  controller  to  verify  the accuracy of the personal data;
texts.text: (a)   the  accuracy  of  the  personal  data  is  contested  by  the  data  subject,  for  a  period  enabling  the  controller  to  verify  the accuracy of the personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   the  processing  is  unlawful  and  the  data  subject  opposes  the  erasure  of  the  personal  data  and  requests  the  restriction of  their  use  instead;
texts.text: (b)   the  processing  is  unlawful  and  the  data  subject  opposes  the  erasure  of  the  personal  data  and  requests  the  restriction of  their  use  instead;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   the  controller  no  longer  needs  the  personal  data  for  the  purposes  of  the  processing,  but  they  are  required  by  the data subject for  the establishment, exercise or defence of  legal claims;
texts.text: (c)   the  controller  no  longer  needs  the  personal  data  for  the  purposes  of  the  processing,  but  they  are  required  by  the data subject for  the establishment, exercise or defence of  legal claims;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d)   the  data  subject  has  objected  to  processing  pursuant  to  Article  21(1)  pending  the  verification  whether  the  legitimate grounds of the controller override those of the data subject.
texts.text: (d)   the  data  subject  has  objected  to  processing  pursuant  to  Article  21(1)  pending  the  verification  whether  the  legitimate grounds of the controller override those of the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where  processing  has  been  restricted  under  paragraph  1,  such  personal  data  shall,  with  the  exception  of  storage, only be  processed  with  the  data  subject's  consent  or  for  the  establishment,  exercise  or  defence  of  legal  claims  or  for  the protection of  the rights of another  natural or legal person or  for  reasons of  important public interest of  the Union or of a Member State.
texts.text: Where  processing  has  been  restricted  under  paragraph  1,  such  personal  data  shall,  with  the  exception  of  storage, only be  processed  with  the  data  subject's  consent  or  for  the  establishment,  exercise  or  defence  of  legal  claims  or  for  the protection of  the rights of another  natural or legal person or  for  reasons of  important public interest of  the Union or of a Member State.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A  data  subject  who  has  obtained  restriction  of  processing  pursuant  to  paragraph  1  shall  be  informed  by  the controller before the restriction of processing is lifted.
texts.text: A  data  subject  who  has  obtained  restriction  of  processing  pursuant  to  paragraph  1  shall  be  informed  by  the controller before the restriction of processing is lifted.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 20
texts.text: Article 20

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to data portability
texts.text: Right to data portability

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to  receive  the  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her,  which  he  or  she  has provided  to  a  controller,  in  a  structured,  commonly  used  and  machine-readable  format  and  have  the  right  to  transmit those  data  to  another  controller  without  hindrance  from  the  controller  to which  the  personal  data  have  been  provided, where:
texts.text: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to  receive  the  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her,  which  he  or  she  has provided  to  a  controller,  in  a  structured,  commonly  used  and  machine-readable  format  and  have  the  right  to  transmit those  data  to  another  controller  without  hindrance  from  the  controller  to which  the  personal  data  have  been  provided, where:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   the  processing  is  based  on  consent  pursuant  to  point  (a)  of  Article  6(1)  or  point  (a)  of  Article  9(2)  or  on  a  contract pursuant to point (b) of Article 6(1); and
texts.text: (a)   the  processing  is  based  on  consent  pursuant  to  point  (a)  of  Article  6(1)  or  point  (a)  of  Article  9(2)  or  on  a  contract pursuant to point (b) of Article 6(1); and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   the  processing is carried out by automated means.
texts.text: (b)   the  processing is carried out by automated means.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In  exercising  his  or  her  right  to  data  portability  pursuant  to  paragraph  1,  the  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to have the personal data transmitted directly from one controller to another, where technically feasible.
texts.text: In  exercising  his  or  her  right  to  data  portability  pursuant  to  paragraph  1,  the  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  to have the personal data transmitted directly from one controller to another, where technically feasible.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  exercise  of  the  right  referred  to  in  paragraph  1  of  this  Article  shall  be  without  prejudice  to  Article  17.  That right  shall  not  apply  to  processing  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  or  in  the exercise of official authority vested in the controller.
texts.text: The  exercise  of  the  right  referred  to  in  paragraph  1  of  this  Article  shall  be  without  prejudice  to  Article  17.  That right  shall  not  apply  to  processing  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task  carried  out  in  the  public  interest  or  in  the exercise of official authority vested in the controller.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The right referred to in paragraph 1 shall not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.
texts.text: The right referred to in paragraph 1 shall not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.

texts.text: Section 4

texts.orig: Article 21
texts.text: Article 21

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to object
texts.text: Right to object

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her  particular situation, at any time to processing  of  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her  which  is  based  on  point  (e)  or  (f)  of  Article  6(1),  including  profiling based  on  those  provisions.  The  controller  shall  no  longer  process  the  personal  data  unless  the  controller  demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds for  the processing which override the interests, rights and freedoms of  the data subject or for  the establishment, exercise or defence of  legal claims.
texts.text: The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her  particular situation, at any time to processing  of  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her  which  is  based  on  point  (e)  or  (f)  of  Article  6(1),  including  profiling based  on  those  provisions.  The  controller  shall  no  longer  process  the  personal  data  unless  the  controller  demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds for  the processing which override the interests, rights and freedoms of  the data subject or for  the establishment, exercise or defence of  legal claims.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal data are processed for direct marketing purposes, the data subject  shall have the right to object  at any  time  to  processing  of  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her  for  such  marketing,  which  includes  profiling  to  the extent that it is related to such direct marketing.
texts.text: Where personal data are processed for direct marketing purposes, the data subject  shall have the right to object  at any  time  to  processing  of  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her  for  such  marketing,  which  includes  profiling  to  the extent that it is related to such direct marketing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where  the  data  subject  objects  to  processing  for  direct  marketing  purposes,  the  personal  data  shall  no  longer  be processed for such purposes.
texts.text: Where  the  data  subject  objects  to  processing  for  direct  marketing  purposes,  the  personal  data  shall  no  longer  be processed for such purposes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: At the latest at the time of  the first communication with the data subject, the right referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2  shall  be  explicitly  brought  to  the  attention  of  the  data  subject  and  shall  be  presented  clearly  and  separately  from  any other information.
texts.text: At the latest at the time of  the first communication with the data subject, the right referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2  shall  be  explicitly  brought  to  the  attention  of  the  data  subject  and  shall  be  presented  clearly  and  separately  from  any other information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In  the  context  of  the  use  of  information  society  services,  and  notwithstanding  Directive  2002/58/EC,  the  data subject may exercise his or her right to object by automated means using technical specifications.
texts.text: In  the  context  of  the  use  of  information  society  services,  and  notwithstanding  Directive  2002/58/EC,  the  data subject may exercise his or her right to object by automated means using technical specifications.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where personal data  are  processed  for  scientific  or  historical  research  purposes  or  statistical  purposes  pursuant  to Article  89(1),  the  data  subject,  on  grounds  relating  to  his  or  her  particular  situation,  shall  have  the  right  to  object  to processing  of  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her,  unless  the  processing  is  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task carried out for reasons of public interest.
texts.text: Where personal data  are  processed  for  scientific  or  historical  research  purposes  or  statistical  purposes  pursuant  to Article  89(1),  the  data  subject,  on  grounds  relating  to  his  or  her  particular  situation,  shall  have  the  right  to  object  to processing  of  personal  data  concerning  him  or  her,  unless  the  processing  is  necessary  for  the  performance  of  a  task carried out for reasons of public interest.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 22
texts.text: Article 22

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
texts.text: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  not  to  be  subject  to  a  decision  based  solely  on  automated  processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her or similarly significantly affects him or her.
texts.text: The  data  subject  shall  have  the  right  not  to  be  subject  to  a  decision  based  solely  on  automated  processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her or similarly significantly affects him or her.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if the decision:
texts.text: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if the decision:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   is  necessary for entering into, or  performance of, a contract between the data subject and a data controller;
texts.text: (a)   is  necessary for entering into, or  performance of, a contract between the data subject and a data controller;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   is  authorised  by  Union  or  Member  State  law  to  which  the  controller  is  subject  and  which  also  lays  down  suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests; or
texts.text: (b)   is  authorised  by  Union  or  Member  State  law  to  which  the  controller  is  subject  and  which  also  lays  down  suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   is  based  on the data subject's explicit consent.
texts.text: (c)   is  based  on the data subject's explicit consent.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In  the  cases  referred  to  in  points  (a)  and  (c)  of  paragraph  2,  the  data  controller  shall  implement  suitable  measures to  safeguard  the  data  subject's rights and  freedoms  and  legitimate  interests,  at  least  the  right  to  obtain  human intervention on the part of the controller, to express his or her point of view and to contest the decision.
texts.text: In  the  cases  referred  to  in  points  (a)  and  (c)  of  paragraph  2,  the  data  controller  shall  implement  suitable  measures to  safeguard  the  data  subject's rights and  freedoms  and  legitimate  interests,  at  least  the  right  to  obtain  human intervention on the part of the controller, to express his or her point of view and to contest the decision.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Decisions  referred  to  in  paragraph  2  shall  not  be  based  on  special  categories  of  personal  data  referred  to  in Article 9(1), unless point (a) or (g) of Article 9(2) applies and suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests are in place.
texts.text: Decisions  referred  to  in  paragraph  2  shall  not  be  based  on  special  categories  of  personal  data  referred  to  in Article 9(1), unless point (a) or (g) of Article 9(2) applies and suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests are in place.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Restrictions
texts.text: Restrictions

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Union or Member State law to which the data controller or processor is subject may restrict by way of a legislative measure the scope of  the obligations and rights provided for  in Articles 12  to 22  and Article 34, as  well as  Article 5  in so  far  as  its  provisions  correspond  to  the  rights  and  obligations  provided  for  in  Articles  12  to  22,  when  such  a restriction  respects  the  essence of  the fundamental  rights  and freedoms and is a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard:
texts.text: Union or Member State law to which the data controller or processor is subject may restrict by way of a legislative measure the scope of  the obligations and rights provided for  in Articles 12  to 22  and Article 34, as  well as  Article 5  in so  far  as  its  provisions  correspond  to  the  rights  and  obligations  provided  for  in  Articles  12  to  22,  when  such  a restriction  respects  the  essence of  the fundamental  rights  and freedoms and is a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard:

texts.text: (a)   national security;

texts.text: (c)   public security;

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: (d)   the  prevention,  investigation,  detection  or  prosecution  of  criminal  offences  or  the  execution  of  criminal  penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e)   other  important  objectives  of  general  public  interest  of  the  Union  or  of  a  Member  State,  in  particular  an  important economic  or  financial  interest  of  the  Union  or  of  a  Member  State,  including  monetary,  budgetary  and  taxation  a matters, public health and social security;
texts.text: (e)   other  important  objectives  of  general  public  interest  of  the  Union  or  of  a  Member  State,  in  particular  an  important economic  or  financial  interest  of  the  Union  or  of  a  Member  State,  including  monetary,  budgetary  and  taxation  a matters, public health and social security;

texts.text: (h)   a  monitoring,  inspection  or  regulatory  function  connected,  even  occasionally,  to  the  exercise  of  official  authority  in the cases referred to in points (a) to (e) and (g);

texts.text: (i) the protection of the data subject or  the rights and freedoms of others;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) the enforcement of civil law claims.
texts.text: (j) the enforcement of civil law claims.

texts.orig: Article 26
texts.text: Article 26

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Joint controllers
texts.text: Joint controllers

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where  two  or  more  controllers  jointly  determine  the  purposes  and  means  of  processing,  they  shall  be  joint controllers.  They  shall  in  a  transparent  manner  determine  their  respective  responsibilities  for  compliance  with  the obligations  under  this  Regulation,  in  particular  as  regards  the  exercising  of  the  rights  of  the  data  subject  and  their respective  duties  to  provide  the  information  referred  to  in  Articles  13  and  14,  by  means  of  an  arrangement  between them unless, and in so far as, the respective responsibilities of  the  controllers  are determined by Union or Member State law to which the controllers are subject. The arrangement may designate a contact point for data subjects.
texts.text: Where  two  or  more  controllers  jointly  determine  the  purposes  and  means  of  processing,  they  shall  be  joint controllers.  They  shall  in  a  transparent  manner  determine  their  respective  responsibilities  for  compliance  with  the obligations  under  this  Regulation,  in  particular  as  regards  the  exercising  of  the  rights  of  the  data  subject  and  their respective  duties  to  provide  the  information  referred  to  in  Articles  13  and  14,  by  means  of  an  arrangement  between them unless, and in so far as, the respective responsibilities of  the  controllers  are determined by Union or Member State law to which the controllers are subject. The arrangement may designate a contact point for data subjects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  arrangement  referred  to  in  paragraph  1  shall  duly  reflect  the  respective  roles  and  relationships  of  the  joint controllers vis-à-vis the data subjects. The essence of the arrangement shall be made available to the data subject.
texts.text: The  arrangement  referred  to  in  paragraph  1  shall  duly  reflect  the  respective  roles  and  relationships  of  the  joint controllers vis-à-vis the data subjects. The essence of the arrangement shall be made available to the data subject.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Irrespective  of  the  terms  of  the  arrangement  referred  to  in  paragraph  1,  the  data  subject  may  exercise  his  or  her rights under  this Regulation in respect of and against each of the controllers.
texts.text: Irrespective  of  the  terms  of  the  arrangement  referred  to  in  paragraph  1,  the  data  subject  may  exercise  his  or  her rights under  this Regulation in respect of and against each of the controllers.

texts.orig: The Member States, the supervisory authorities, the Board and the Commission shall encourage the drawing up of codes  of  conduct  intended  to  contribute  to  the  proper  application  of  this  Regulation,  taking  account  of  the  specific features of  the various processing sectors and the specific needs of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises.
texts.text: The Member States, the supervisory authorities, the Board and the Commission shall encourage the drawing up of codes  of  conduct  intended  to  contribute  to  the  proper  application  of  this  Regulation,  taking  account  of  the  specific features of  the various processing sectors and the specific needs of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Associations  and  other  bodies  representing  categories  of  controllers  or  processors  may  prepare  codes  of  conduct, or amend or extend such codes, for the purpose of specifying the application of this Regulation, such as with regard to:
texts.text: Associations  and  other  bodies  representing  categories  of  controllers  or  processors  may  prepare  codes  of  conduct, or amend or extend such codes, for the purpose of specifying the application of this Regulation, such as with regard to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   fair  and  transparent processing;
texts.text: (a)   fair  and  transparent processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   the  legitimate interests pursued by controllers in specific contexts;
texts.text: (b)   the  legitimate interests pursued by controllers in specific contexts;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   the  collection of personal data;
texts.text: (c)   the  collection of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d)   the  pseudonymisation of personal data;
texts.text: (d)   the  pseudonymisation of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e)   the  information provided to the public and to data subjects;
texts.text: (e)   the  information provided to the public and to data subjects;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f)   the exercise of  the rights of data subjects;
texts.text: (f)   the exercise of  the rights of data subjects;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g)   the  information provided  to, and  the protection of, children,  and the  manner  in which the  consent of  the  holders of parental responsibility over children is to be obtained;
texts.text: (g)   the  information provided  to, and  the protection of, children,  and the  manner  in which the  consent of  the  holders of parental responsibility over children is to be obtained;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h)   the  measures  and  procedures  referred  to  in  Articles  24  and  25  and  the  measures  to  ensure  security  of  processing referred to in Article 32;
texts.text: (h)   the  measures  and  procedures  referred  to  in  Articles  24  and  25  and  the  measures  to  ensure  security  of  processing referred to in Article 32;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) the  notification  of  personal  data  breaches  to  supervisory  authorities  and  the  communication  of  such  personal  data breaches to data subjects;
texts.text: (i) the  notification  of  personal  data  breaches  to  supervisory  authorities  and  the  communication  of  such  personal  data breaches to data subjects;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations; or
texts.text: (j) the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (k)   out-of-court proceedings and other dispute resolution procedures for resolving disputes between controllers and data subjects with regard to processing, without prejudice to the rights of data subjects pursuant to Articles 77 and 79.
texts.text: (k)   out-of-court proceedings and other dispute resolution procedures for resolving disputes between controllers and data subjects with regard to processing, without prejudice to the rights of data subjects pursuant to Articles 77 and 79.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In  addition  to  adherence  by  controllers  or  processors  subject  to  this  Regulation,  codes  of  conduct  approved pursuant  to  paragraph  5  of  this  Article  and  having  general  validity  pursuant  to  paragraph  9  of  this  Article  may  also  be adhered to by controllers  or  processors  that  are  not  subject  to  this  Regulation  pursuant  to  Article  3  in  order  to  provide appropriate  safeguards  within  the  framework of  personal  data  transfers  to  third  countries  or  international  organisations under  the  terms  referred  to  in  point  (e)  of  Article  46(2).  Such  controllers  or  processors  shall  make  binding  and enforceable  commitments,  via  contractual  or  other  legally  binding  instruments,  to  apply  those  appropriate  safeguards including with regard to the rights of data subjects.
texts.text: In  addition  to  adherence  by  controllers  or  processors  subject  to  this  Regulation,  codes  of  conduct  approved pursuant  to  paragraph  5  of  this  Article  and  having  general  validity  pursuant  to  paragraph  9  of  this  Article  may  also  be adhered to by controllers  or  processors  that  are  not  subject  to  this  Regulation  pursuant  to  Article  3  in  order  to  provide appropriate  safeguards  within  the  framework of  personal  data  transfers  to  third  countries  or  international  organisations under  the  terms  referred  to  in  point  (e)  of  Article  46(2).  Such  controllers  or  processors  shall  make  binding  and enforceable  commitments,  via  contractual  or  other  legally  binding  instruments,  to  apply  those  appropriate  safeguards including with regard to the rights of data subjects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A  code  of  conduct  referred  to  in  paragraph  2  of  this  Article  shall  contain  mechanisms  which  enable  the  body referred  to  in  Article  41(1)  to  carry  out  the  mandatory  monitoring  of  compliance  with  its  provisions  by  the  controllers or  processors  which  undertake  to  apply  it,  without  prejudice  to  the  tasks  and  powers  of  supervisory  authorities competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56.
texts.text: A  code  of  conduct  referred  to  in  paragraph  2  of  this  Article  shall  contain  mechanisms  which  enable  the  body referred  to  in  Article  41(1)  to  carry  out  the  mandatory  monitoring  of  compliance  with  its  provisions  by  the  controllers or  processors  which  undertake  to  apply  it,  without  prejudice  to  the  tasks  and  powers  of  supervisory  authorities competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Associations and other bodies referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article which intend to prepare a code of conduct or  to  amend  or  extend  an  existing  code  shall  submit  the  draft  code,  amendment  or  extension  to  the  supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55. The supervisory authority shall provide an opinion on whether  the draft  code,  amendment  or  extension  complies  with  this  Regulation  and  shall  approve  that  draft  code,  amendment  or extension if it finds that it provides sufficient appropriate safeguards.
texts.text: Associations and other bodies referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article which intend to prepare a code of conduct or  to  amend  or  extend  an  existing  code  shall  submit  the  draft  code,  amendment  or  extension  to  the  supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55. The supervisory authority shall provide an opinion on whether  the draft  code,  amendment  or  extension  complies  with  this  Regulation  and  shall  approve  that  draft  code,  amendment  or extension if it finds that it provides sufficient appropriate safeguards.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where  the  draft  code,  or  amendment  or  extension  is  approved  in  accordance  with  paragraph  5,  and  where  the code  of  conduct  concerned  does  not  relate  to  processing  activities  in  several  Member  States,  the  supervisory  authority shall register and publish the code.
texts.text: Where  the  draft  code,  or  amendment  or  extension  is  approved  in  accordance  with  paragraph  5,  and  where  the code  of  conduct  concerned  does  not  relate  to  processing  activities  in  several  Member  States,  the  supervisory  authority shall register and publish the code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where  a  draft  code  of  conduct  relates  to  processing  activities  in  several  Member  States,  the  supervisory  authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55 shall, before approving the draft code, amendment or extension, submit it in the  procedure  referred  to  in  Article  63  to  the  Board  which  shall  provide  an  opinion  on  whether  the  draft  code, amendment  or  extension  complies  with  this  Regulation  or,  in  the  situation  referred  to  in  paragraph  3  of  this  Article, provides appropriate safeguards.
texts.text: Where  a  draft  code  of  conduct  relates  to  processing  activities  in  several  Member  States,  the  supervisory  authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55 shall, before approving the draft code, amendment or extension, submit it in the  procedure  referred  to  in  Article  63  to  the  Board  which  shall  provide  an  opinion  on  whether  the  draft  code, amendment  or  extension  complies  with  this  Regulation  or,  in  the  situation  referred  to  in  paragraph  3  of  this  Article, provides appropriate safeguards.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where  the  opinion  referred  to  in  paragraph  7  confirms  that  the  draft  code,  amendment  or  extension  complies with  this  Regulation,  or,  in  the  situation  referred  to  in  paragraph  3,  provides  appropriate  safeguards,  the  Board  shall submit its opinion to the Commission.
texts.text: Where  the  opinion  referred  to  in  paragraph  7  confirms  that  the  draft  code,  amendment  or  extension  complies with  this  Regulation,  or,  in  the  situation  referred  to  in  paragraph  3,  provides  appropriate  safeguards,  the  Board  shall submit its opinion to the Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Commission  may,  by  way  of  implementing  acts,  decide  that  the  approved  code  of  conduct,  amendment  or extension  submitted  to  it  pursuant  to  paragraph  8  of  this  Article  have  general  validity  within  the  Union.  Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure set out in Article 93(2).
texts.text: The  Commission  may,  by  way  of  implementing  acts,  decide  that  the  approved  code  of  conduct,  amendment  or extension  submitted  to  it  pursuant  to  paragraph  8  of  this  Article  have  general  validity  within  the  Union.  Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure set out in Article 93(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Commission  shall  ensure  appropriate  publicity  for  the  approved  codes  which  have  been  decided  as  having general validity in accordance with paragraph 9.
texts.text: The  Commission  shall  ensure  appropriate  publicity  for  the  approved  codes  which  have  been  decided  as  having general validity in accordance with paragraph 9.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Board  shall  collate  all  approved  codes  of  conduct,  amendments  and  extensions  in  a  register  and  shall  make them publicly available by way of appropriate means.
texts.text: The  Board  shall  collate  all  approved  codes  of  conduct,  amendments  and  extensions  in  a  register  and  shall  make them publicly available by way of appropriate means.

texts.orig: Article 42
texts.text: Article 42

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Certification
texts.text: Certification

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Member  States,  the  supervisory  authorities,  the  Board  and  the  Commission  shall  encourage,  in  particular  at Union  level,  the  establishment  of  data  protection  certification  mechanisms  and  of  data  protection  seals  and  marks,  for the  purpose  of  demonstrating  compliance  with  this  Regulation  of  processing  operations  by  controllers  and  processors. The specific needs of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises shall be taken into account.
texts.text: The  Member  States,  the  supervisory  authorities,  the  Board  and  the  Commission  shall  encourage,  in  particular  at Union  level,  the  establishment  of  data  protection  certification  mechanisms  and  of  data  protection  seals  and  marks,  for the  purpose  of  demonstrating  compliance  with  this  Regulation  of  processing  operations  by  controllers  and  processors. The specific needs of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises shall be taken into account.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In  addition  to  adherence  by  controllers  or  processors  subject  to  this  Regulation,  data  protection  certification mechanisms,  seals  or  marks  approved  pursuant  to  paragraph  5  of  this  Article  may  be  established  for  the  purpose  of demonstrating  the  existence  of  appropriate  safeguards  provided  by controllers  or  processors  that  are  not  subject  to  this Regulation  pursuant  to  Article  3  within  the  framework  of  personal  data  transfers  to  third  countries  or  international organisations under  the terms referred to in point (f) of Article 46(2). Such controllers or  processors shall make binding and enforceable commitments,  via  contractual or other legally binding instruments, to apply those appropriate safeguards, including with regard to the rights of data subjects.
texts.text: In  addition  to  adherence  by  controllers  or  processors  subject  to  this  Regulation,  data  protection  certification mechanisms,  seals  or  marks  approved  pursuant  to  paragraph  5  of  this  Article  may  be  established  for  the  purpose  of demonstrating  the  existence  of  appropriate  safeguards  provided  by controllers  or  processors  that  are  not  subject  to  this Regulation  pursuant  to  Article  3  within  the  framework  of  personal  data  transfers  to  third  countries  or  international organisations under  the terms referred to in point (f) of Article 46(2). Such controllers or  processors shall make binding and enforceable commitments,  via  contractual or other legally binding instruments, to apply those appropriate safeguards, including with regard to the rights of data subjects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The certification shall be voluntary and available via a process that is transparent.
texts.text: The certification shall be voluntary and available via a process that is transparent.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A  certification  pursuant  to  this  Article  does  not  reduce  the  responsibility  of  the  controller  or  the  processor  for compliance  with  this  Regulation  and  is  without  prejudice  to  the  tasks  and  powers  of  the  supervisory  authorities  which are competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56.
texts.text: A  certification  pursuant  to  this  Article  does  not  reduce  the  responsibility  of  the  controller  or  the  processor  for compliance  with  this  Regulation  and  is  without  prejudice  to  the  tasks  and  powers  of  the  supervisory  authorities  which are competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A certification pursuant to this Article shall be issued by the certification bodies referred to in Article 43 or by the competent supervisory authority, on the  basis  of  criteria  approved  by  that  competent  supervisory  authority  pursuant  to Article  58(3)  or  by  the  Board  pursuant  to Article  63.  Where  the  criteria  are  approved  by the  Board,  this  may  result  in  a common certification, the European Data Protection Seal.
texts.text: A certification pursuant to this Article shall be issued by the certification bodies referred to in Article 43 or by the competent supervisory authority, on the  basis  of  criteria  approved  by  that  competent  supervisory  authority  pursuant  to Article  58(3)  or  by  the  Board  pursuant  to Article  63.  Where  the  criteria  are  approved  by the  Board,  this  may  result  in  a common certification, the European Data Protection Seal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  controller  or  processor  which  submits  its  processing  to  the  certification  mechanism  shall  provide  the  certifi­ cation  body  referred  to  in  Article  43,  or  where  applicable,  the  competent  supervisory  authority,  with  all  information and access to its processing activities which are necessary to conduct the certification procedure.
texts.text: The  controller  or  processor  which  submits  its  processing  to  the  certification  mechanism  shall  provide  the  certifi­ cation  body  referred  to  in  Article  43,  or  where  applicable,  the  competent  supervisory  authority,  with  all  information and access to its processing activities which are necessary to conduct the certification procedure.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Certification  shall  be  issued  to  a  controller  or  processor  for  a  maximum  period  of  three  years  and  may  be renewed,  under  the  same  conditions,  provided  that  the  relevant  requirements  continue  to  be  met.  Certification  shall  be withdrawn, as applicable, by the certification bodies referred to in Article 43  or by the competent supervisory authority where the requirements for the certification are not or are no longer met.
texts.text: Certification  shall  be  issued  to  a  controller  or  processor  for  a  maximum  period  of  three  years  and  may  be renewed,  under  the  same  conditions,  provided  that  the  relevant  requirements  continue  to  be  met.  Certification  shall  be withdrawn, as applicable, by the certification bodies referred to in Article 43  or by the competent supervisory authority where the requirements for the certification are not or are no longer met.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Board  shall  collate  all  certification  mechanisms  and  data  protection  seals  and  marks  in  a  register  and  shall make them publicly available by any appropriate means.
texts.text: The  Board  shall  collate  all  certification  mechanisms  and  data  protection  seals  and  marks  in  a  register  and  shall make them publicly available by any appropriate means.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 45
texts.text: Article 45

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Transfers on the basis of an adequacy decision
texts.text: Transfers on the basis of an adequacy decision

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A  transfer  of  personal  data  to  a  third  country  or  an  international  organisation  may  take  place  where  the Commission has decided that the third country, a territory or one or more specified sectors within that third country, or the  international  organisation  in  question  ensures  an  adequate  level  of  protection.  Such  a  transfer  shall  not  require  any specific authorisation.
texts.text: A  transfer  of  personal  data  to  a  third  country  or  an  international  organisation  may  take  place  where  the Commission has decided that the third country, a territory or one or more specified sectors within that third country, or the  international  organisation  in  question  ensures  an  adequate  level  of  protection.  Such  a  transfer  shall  not  require  any specific authorisation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When  assessing  the  adequacy  of  the  level  of  protection,  the  Commission  shall,  in  particular,  take  account  of  the following elements:
texts.text: When  assessing  the  adequacy  of  the  level  of  protection,  the  Commission  shall,  in  particular,  take  account  of  the following elements:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a)   the  rule  of  law,  respect  for  human  rights  and  fundamental  freedoms,  relevant  legislation,  both  general  and  sectoral, including concerning public security, defence, national security and criminal law and the access of public authorities to  personal  data,  as  well  as  the  implementation  of  such  legislation,  data  protection  rules,  professional  rules  and security  measures,  including  rules  for  the  onward  transfer  of  personal  data  to another  third  country or  international organisation which are complied with in that country or  international organisation, case-law, as well as effective and enforceable  data  subject  rights  and  effective  administrative  and  judicial  redress  for  the  data  subjects  whose  personal data are being transferred;
texts.text: (a)   the  rule  of  law,  respect  for  human  rights  and  fundamental  freedoms,  relevant  legislation,  both  general  and  sectoral, including concerning public security, defence, national security and criminal law and the access of public authorities to  personal  data,  as  well  as  the  implementation  of  such  legislation,  data  protection  rules,  professional  rules  and security  measures,  including  rules  for  the  onward  transfer  of  personal  data  to another  third  country or  international organisation which are complied with in that country or  international organisation, case-law, as well as effective and enforceable  data  subject  rights  and  effective  administrative  and  judicial  redress  for  the  data  subjects  whose  personal data are being transferred;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b)   the  existence and effective functioning of one or more independent supervisory authorities in the third country or  to which  an  international  organisation  is  subject,  with  responsibility  for  ensuring  and  enforcing  compliance  with  the data  protection  rules,  including  adequate  enforcement  powers,  for  assisting  and  advising  the  data  subjects  in exercising their  rights and for cooperation with the supervisory authorities of the Member States; and
texts.text: (b)   the  existence and effective functioning of one or more independent supervisory authorities in the third country or  to which  an  international  organisation  is  subject,  with  responsibility  for  ensuring  and  enforcing  compliance  with  the data  protection  rules,  including  adequate  enforcement  powers,  for  assisting  and  advising  the  data  subjects  in exercising their  rights and for cooperation with the supervisory authorities of the Member States; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c)   the  international  commitments  the  third  country  or  international  organisation  concerned  has  entered  into,  or  other obligations  arising  from  legally  binding  conventions  or  instruments  as  well  as  from  its  participation  in  multilateral or  regional systems, in particular  in relation to the protection of personal data.
texts.text: (c)   the  international  commitments  the  third  country  or  international  organisation  concerned  has  entered  into,  or  other obligations  arising  from  legally  binding  conventions  or  instruments  as  well  as  from  its  participation  in  multilateral or  regional systems, in particular  in relation to the protection of personal data.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Commission,  after  assessing  the  adequacy  of  the  level  of  protection,  may  decide,  by  means  of  implementing act,  that  a  third  country,  a  territory  or  one  or  more  specified  sectors  within  a  third  country,  or  an  international organisation ensures an adequate level of protection within the meaning  of paragraph 2 of this Article. The implementing  act  shall  provide  for  a  mechanism  for  a  periodic  review,  at  least  every  four  years,  which  shall  take  into account all  relevant  developments  in  the  third  country or  international  organisation.  The  implementing  act  shall  specify its  territorial  and  sectoral  application  and,  where  applicable,  identify  the  supervisory  authority  or  authorities  referred  to in  point  (b)  of  paragraph  2  of  this  Article.  The  implementing  act  shall  be  adopted  in  accordance  with  the  examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).
texts.text: The  Commission,  after  assessing  the  adequacy  of  the  level  of  protection,  may  decide,  by  means  of  implementing act,  that  a  third  country,  a  territory  or  one  or  more  specified  sectors  within  a  third  country,  or  an  international organisation ensures an adequate level of protection within the meaning  of paragraph 2 of this Article. The implementing  act  shall  provide  for  a  mechanism  for  a  periodic  review,  at  least  every  four  years,  which  shall  take  into account all  relevant  developments  in  the  third  country or  international  organisation.  The  implementing  act  shall  specify its  territorial  and  sectoral  application  and,  where  applicable,  identify  the  supervisory  authority  or  authorities  referred  to in  point  (b)  of  paragraph  2  of  this  Article.  The  implementing  act  shall  be  adopted  in  accordance  with  the  examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Commission  shall,  on  an  ongoing  basis,  monitor  developments  in  third  countries  and  international  organ­ isations  that  could  affect  the  functioning  of  decisions  adopted  pursuant  to  paragraph  3  of  this  Article  and  decisions adopted on the basis of Article 25(6) of Directive 95/46/EC.
texts.text: The  Commission  shall,  on  an  ongoing  basis,  monitor  developments  in  third  countries  and  international  organ­ isations  that  could  affect  the  functioning  of  decisions  adopted  pursuant  to  paragraph  3  of  this  Article  and  decisions adopted on the basis of Article 25(6) of Directive 95/46/EC.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Commission  shall,  where  available  information  reveals,  in  particular  following  the  review  referred  to  in paragraph  3  of  this  Article,  that  a  third  country,  a  territory  or  one  or  more  specified  sectors  within  a  third  country,  or an  international  organisation  no  longer  ensures  an  adequate  level  of  protection  within  the  meaning  of  paragraph  2  of this  Article,  to  the  extent  necessary,  repeal,  amend  or  suspend  the  decision  referred  to  in  paragraph  3  of  this  Article  by means  of  implementing  acts  without  retro-active  effect.  Those  implementing  acts  shall  be  adopted  in  accordance  with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).
texts.text: The  Commission  shall,  where  available  information  reveals,  in  particular  following  the  review  referred  to  in paragraph  3  of  this  Article,  that  a  third  country,  a  territory  or  one  or  more  specified  sectors  within  a  third  country,  or an  international  organisation  no  longer  ensures  an  adequate  level  of  protection  within  the  meaning  of  paragraph  2  of this  Article,  to  the  extent  necessary,  repeal,  amend  or  suspend  the  decision  referred  to  in  paragraph  3  of  this  Article  by means  of  implementing  acts  without  retro-active  effect.  Those  implementing  acts  shall  be  adopted  in  accordance  with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On duly justified  imperative  grounds  of  urgency,  the  Commission  shall  adopt immediately  applicable  implementing  acts in accordance with the procedure referred to in Article 93(3).
texts.text: On duly justified  imperative  grounds  of  urgency,  the  Commission  shall  adopt immediately  applicable  implementing  acts in accordance with the procedure referred to in Article 93(3).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall enter  into consultations  with  the  third  country or  international  organisation with  a  view  to remedying the situation giving rise to the decision made pursuant to paragraph 5.
texts.text: The Commission shall enter  into consultations  with  the  third  country or  international  organisation with  a  view  to remedying the situation giving rise to the decision made pursuant to paragraph 5.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A  decision  pursuant  to  paragraph  5  of  this  Article  is  without  prejudice  to  transfers  of  personal  data  to  the  third country,  a  territory  or  one  or  more  specified  sectors  within  that  third  country,  or  the  international  organisation  in question pursuant to Articles 46 to 49.
texts.text: A  decision  pursuant  to  paragraph  5  of  this  Article  is  without  prejudice  to  transfers  of  personal  data  to  the  third country,  a  territory  or  one  or  more  specified  sectors  within  that  third  country,  or  the  international  organisation  in question pursuant to Articles 46 to 49.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The  Commission  shall  publish  in  the Official  Journal  of  the  European  Union and  on  its  website  a  list  of  the  third countries, territories and specified sectors within a third country and international organisations for  which it has decided that an adequate level of protection is or is no longer ensured.
texts.text: The  Commission  shall  publish  in  the Official  Journal  of  the  European  Union and  on  its  website  a  list  of  the  third countries, territories and specified sectors within a third country and international organisations for  which it has decided that an adequate level of protection is or is no longer ensured.
